title: Breitmann as a Bummer
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Sheneral Sherman holts oop on his coorse,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He shtops at de gross-road und reins in his horse.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dereן¿½s a ford on de rifer dis day we moost dake,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or elshe de grand army in bieces shall preak!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhen shoost ash dis vord from his lips had gone bast,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ There coomed a young orterly gallopinן¿½ fast,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Who gry mit amazement: ן¿½Herr Shenן¿½ral! Goot Lord!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat Bummer der Breitmann ish holdinן¿½ der ford!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Shenן¿½ral he ootered no hymn und no psalm,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But opened his lips und he priefly say ן¿½Dן¿½n!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere moost hafe been viskey on dat side der rifer;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To get it dose shaps vould set hell in a shiver;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But now dat dey hold it, ride quick to deir aid:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ho, Sickles! move prompן¿½ly, send down a prigade!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat Dootchman moost vork mighty hard mit his sword
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ If againsd a whole army he holds to de ford.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey spoored on, dey hooryן¿½d on, gallopinן¿½ shtraight,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But for Breitmann help coomed shoost a liddle too late,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For as de Lauwinן¿½ goes smash mit her pound,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So on to de Bummers de repels coom down:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Heinrich von Schinkensteinן¿½s tead in de road,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dieterich Hinkelbeinן¿½s flat as a toad;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und Sepperl Tyroler shpoke nefer a vord,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But shoost ן¿½Mutter Gottes!ן¿½ und died in de ford.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Itschן¿½l of Innspruck ish drilled droo de hair,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Einer aus Bן¿½blingen he too vash dere
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Karli of Karlisruhן¿½s shot near de fence
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (His horse vash oן¿½erloadet mit toorkies und hens),
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dough he like a ravinן¿½ mad cannibal fought
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Yet der Breitmann der captן¿½n der hero vash caught;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und de last dings ve saw, he vas tied mit a cord,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For de repels had goppled him oop at de ford.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey shtripped off his goat und skyugled his poots
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey dressed him mit rags of a repel recruits;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But von gray-haared oldt veller shmiled crimly und bet
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat Breitmann vouldt be a pad egg for dem yet.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He has more on his pipe as dem vellers allows,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He has cardts yet in hand und das Spiel ist nicht aus,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Deyן¿½ll find dat dey took in der Teufel to board,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De day dey pooled Breitmann vell ofer de ford.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In de Bowery each beer-haus mit crape vas oopdone,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhen dey read in de papers dat Breitmann vas gone;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und de Dootch all cot troonk oopon lager und wein,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At the great Trauer-fest of de Turner Verein.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere vas wein en mit weinen ven beoplesh did dink
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat Shermanן¿½s great Sharman cood nefer more trink.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und in Villiam Shtreet veepinן¿½ und vailenן¿½ vas hoorן¿½d,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Pecause der Hans Breitmann vas lost at de ford.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ SECOND PART

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In dulce jubilo now ve all sings,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A-vaifinן¿½ de panners like efery dings.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De preeze droo de bine-trees ish cooler und salt,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und der Shenן¿½ral is merry venefer ve halt;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Loosty und merry he schmells at de preeze,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Lustig und heiter he looks droo de drees,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Lustig und heiter ash vell he may pe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For Sherman, at last has marched down to the sea.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½s a gry from de guart dereן¿½s a clotter und dramp,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhen dat fery same orterly rides droo de camp
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Who report on de ford. Dere ish droples and awe
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In de face of de youfן¿½ apout somedings he saw;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und he shpeak me in Frן¿½ntsch, like he always do: ן¿½Look!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Sagre pleu! Fentre Tieu! dere ish Breitmann his spook!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He ish goming dis vay! Nom de Garce! can it pe
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat de spooks of de tead men coom down to de sea!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und he looks, und ve sees, und ve tremples mit tread,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For risinן¿½ all swart on de efeninן¿½ red
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vas Johannes der Breitmann der war es, bei Gott!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Coom ridingן¿½ to oos-vard, right shtraight to de shpot!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ All mouse-still ve shtood, yet mit oop-shoompinן¿½ hearts,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For he look shoost so pig as de shiant of de Hartz;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und I heard de Sout Deutschers say ן¿½Ave Morie!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Braise Gott all goot shpirids py land und by sea!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot Itzig of Frankfort he lift oop his nose,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und be-mark dat de shpook hat peen changinן¿½ his clothes,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For he seemed like an Generalissimus drest
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In a vlaminן¿½ new coat und magnificent vest.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Six bistols beschlagen mit silber he vore,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und a cold mounded swordt like a Kaisar he bore,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und ve dinks dat de ghosdt or votever he peן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Moost hafe proken some panks on his vay to de sea.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Id is he!ן¿½ן¿½Und er lebt noch!ן¿½ he lifes ve all say:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Der Breitmann Oldt Breitmann! Hans Breitmann! Herr Je!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und ve roosh to emprace him, und shtill more ve find
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat vherefer heן¿½d peen, heן¿½d left noding pehine.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In bofe of his poots dere vas porte-moneys crammed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit creen-packs stoof full all his haversack jammed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In his bockets cold dollars vere shinglinן¿½ deir doons
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit dwo doozen votches und four dozen shpoons,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dwo silber tea-pods for makinן¿½ his dea,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der ghosdt hafe pring mit him, en route to de sea.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit goot sweed-botatoes, und doorkies, und rice,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ve makes him a sooper of efery dings nice.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und de bummers hoont roundt apout, alle wie ein,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dill dey findt a plantaschion mit parrels of wein.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Den tן¿½vas ן¿½Hereן¿½s to you, Breitmann! Alt Schwedן¿½ bist zurן¿½ck?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot teufels you makes since dis fourteen nights veek?ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und ve holds von shtupendous and derriple shpree
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For shoy dat der Breitmann has got to de sea.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But in fain tid we ashk vhere der Breitmann hat peen,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot he tid; vot he pass droo or vot he might seen?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere he kits his vine horse, or who gafe him dem woons,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und how Brovidence plessed him mit tea-pods und shpoons?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For to all of dem queeries he only reblies,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½If you dells me no quesdions, I ashks you no lies!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So ן¿½Twas glear dat some derriple myshן¿½dry moost pe
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere he kits all dat ploonder he prings to de sea.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere ish bapers in Richmond dells derriple lies
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How Shermanן¿½s grand armee hafe raise deir sooplies:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For ve readt in brindt dat der Sheneral Grant
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Say de bummers hafe only shoost take vat dey vant.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But ן¿½Tis vhispered dat vhile a refolferן¿½ll go round
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der BREITMANN vill nefer a pegginן¿½ be found;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or shtarvinן¿½ ash brisner by doonder! not he,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile der Teufel could help him to ged to de sea.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Charles G. Leland's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
